title: Paper clay hanging wall stars
subtitle: A set of three textured stars cut from rolled paper clay and hung on cord
excerpt: Three stars cut from rolled paper clay, textured, pierced, and dried flat. Paper clay air-dries without cracking at the thin points where star arms meet the body; plain air-dry clay would crack here. Seal and hang on waxed cotton cord as wall decorations.
sourceNotes: Paper clay slab construction derived from first principles.

content.text: Paper clay is the better material for stars and other shapes with thin points: the cellulose fibres bridge the narrow sections where star arms taper to a point, which plain air-dry clay usually cracks through. Cut, texture, pierce, and let them dry flat. Once
, seal and hang in groups of three on a branch or cord.

items.name: Star cutter (three sizes)
items.substitution: Cut around a card template with a craft knife

content.text: to 5 mm thickness. Let it rest 5 minutes on the board before cutting.
Press a lace square, fabric, or stamp into the slab surface while still soft. Roll lightly over the texture piece with the rolling pin for an even impression.

Cut one large star (about 10 cm), one medium (7 cm), and one small (5 cm) from the slab. Press the cutters straight down without dragging. Remove the cutter and lift the star off the board with a thin palette knife.

Pierce a hole 4 mm from the top point of each star with the needle tool. Twist gently to enlarge to about 3 mm diameter. Make the hole while the clay is still soft; piercing bone-dry paper clay can chip the point.

Dry flat on the board. Cover loosely with plastic for the first 4 hours to slow the surface. Once bone-dry (one to two days), sand any rough edges lightly. Seal with PVA. Paint with acrylics or leave natural, then seal. Thread waxed cord through each hole and tie the stars at different heights on a horizontal branch.

items.fix: Use paper clay; cover tips with a small strip of plastic while drying
items.cause: Plain air-dry clay used instead of paper clay, or drying too fast
items.symptom: Star arms crack at the tips

items.fix: Dry on a plaster bat or flat board; place a light tile on top for the first hour to keep them flat
items.cause: Dried on a non-flat surface or one side drying faster
items.symptom: Stars warp and curve as they dry
